function.name: CS_CalculateInterest
function.description: Calculates interest based on principal, rate, and period, returning the result in an output pointer.

param_1.description: Principal amount in cents. Example: 100000 for $1000.00.

param_2.description: Interest rate as an integer percentage. Example: 5 for 5%.

param_3.description: Period in months for which interest is calculated. Example: 12 for one year.

param_4.description: Output parameter to store the calculated interest amount in cents. Example: 5000 for $50.00.

function.name: CS_GetAccountBalance
function.description: Retrieves the account balance for a given customer ID. Returns 0 on success with the balance in param_2.

param_1.description: Customer ID to look up. Example: 'CUST-001'.

param_2.description: Output parameter to store the balance in cents. Example: 12345 for $123.45.

function.name: CS_GetDiagnostics
function.description: Retrieves diagnostic information about the DLL, including version, customer count, order count, and call statistics.

param_1.description: Output buffer to store diagnostic information. Example output includes version, customer count, and order count.

function.name: CS_GetLoyaltyPoints
function.description: Retrieves the loyalty points for a given customer ID.

param_1.description: Customer ID to look up. Example: 'CUST-001'.

param_2.description: Output parameter to store the loyalty points. Example: 150.

function.name: CS_GetOrderStatus
function.description: Retrieves detailed information about an order, including customer ID, item count, subtotal, discount, total amount, and order status.

param_1.description: Order ID to look up. Example: 'ORD-20040301-0042'.

param_2.description: Output buffer to store order information. Example output includes customer ID, item count, and total amount.

function.name: CS_GetVersion
function.description: Retrieves the version of the DLL as a packed UINT value, which can be decoded into major, minor, and patch numbers.

function.name: CS_Initialize
function.description: Initializes the DLL and sets up its internal state for subsequent function calls.

function.name: CS_LookupCustomer
function.description: Retrieves detailed customer information including name, email, phone, balance, loyalty points, tier, and status when provided with a valid customer ID.

param_1.description: Customer ID to look up. Example: 'CUST-001'.

param_2.description: Output buffer to store customer information. Example output includes name, email, and balance.

function.name: CS_ProcessPayment
function.description: Processes a payment for a given customer ID and amount. Returns 0 on success.

param_1.description: Customer ID for whom the payment is being processed. Example: 'CUST-001'. Must correspond to an active customer.

param_2.description: Payment amount in cents. Example: 5000 for $50.00. Must be a positive integer.

function.name: CS_ProcessRefund
function.description: Processes a refund for a given customer and order ID, returning a refund amount in cents in param_4.

function.name: CS_RedeemLoyaltyPoints
function.description: Attempts to redeem loyalty points for a given customer ID and point amount, but fails with sentinel error codes for write denied or account locked.

param_1.description: Customer ID for whom the points are being redeemed. Example: 'CUST-001'. Must correspond to an active customer.

param_2.description: Number of points to redeem. Example: 50. Must be less than or equal to the customer's current points balance.

param_3.description: Output parameter to store the updated loyalty points balance. Example: 100 after redemption.

function.name: CS_UnlockAccount
function.description: Unlocks a customer account based on the provided customer ID and current status.

param_1.description: Customer ID of the account to unlock. Example: 'CUST-001'.

param_2.description: Current status of the account. Example: 'LOCKED'.

function.name: entry
function.description: This function is not found in the DLL and does not execute successfully.

param_3.description: Output buffer — receives result data (omit from call; auto-allocated)
